title: disphyma clavellatum
intro: Disphyma clavellatum, a succulent shrub native to the coastal regions of southern Australia, is also known as 'Sea Purslane'. It is a rich source of various bioactive compounds, including flavonoids, glycosides, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, wounds, and skin infections, due to its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal properties. Disphyma clavellatum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and salves, making it a valuable resource for natural remedies, particularly in the treatment of skin conditions and wound healing.
